mitigations: Add business-impact-specific controls (approval, segregation of duties, transaction limits) given the high business/regulatory impact (Q87).
Define retention limits and deletion controls for prompts, responses, memory, logs, and vector data (Q88).
Add replay protection: nonces/idempotency keys and fresh authorization for state-changing or sensitive actions (Q91).
Introduce adversarial-prompt / jailbreak / tool-misuse regression testing (Q90).
Enforce tenant isolation and blast-radius reduction (scoped credentials, per-tenant limits) for the multi-tenant/public scale (Q86).
Use strict parsers, file validation, sandboxing, content normalization, and instruction/data isolation for the risky input formats (Q83/Q84).
Require authentication and least privilege on the public-facing surface.
Reduce exposure and add authentication, rate limiting, and security monitoring for exposed interfaces (affected: Web Chat Interface, REST API Endpoint, Third-party Integration).
Enforce server-side authorization on every request and object; deny by default.
Apply least privilege across roles and resources.

quick_wins: Add server-side authorization checks on every endpoint and object.
Disable debug/verbose errors and harden default configs.
Enforce TLS everywhere and encrypt sensitive data at rest.
Use parameterized queries and strict input validation.
Enable MFA and harden session/credential handling.
Verify signatures/integrity of updates and critical data.
Centralize security logging and alert on anomalies.
